name: St. Mark's Bell Tower (Campanile)
overview: The Campanile di San Marco is Venice's most recognizable landmark, a tall brick bell tower that offers spectacular panoramic views of the city, lagoon, and surrounding islands. It serves as the bell tower for St. Mark's Basilica.
history: The original tower was built in the 9th century and rebuilt several times. The current tower was completed in 1173 but dramatically collapsed in 1902. It was faithfully reconstructed and reopened in 1912 using the same materials and design.
highlights: 98.6-meter height offering Venice's best panoramic views
Elevator ride to the top (no stairs required for tourists)
Views of St. Mark's Square, Grand Canal, and Doge's Palace from above
Five historic bells, each with different names and purposes
tips: Visit early morning or late afternoon to avoid crowds and get the best lighting for photos. The elevator makes it accessible for all ages. Clear days offer views all the way to the Dolomites. Combine with St. Mark's Basilica visit.
